texto: These maxims and the art of interpreting them may be said to constitute the premisses of science but I prefer to call them our scientific beliefs. These premisses or beliefs are embodied in a tradition, the tradition of science.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Polanyi sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “These maxims and the art of interpreting them may be said to constitute the premisses of science …” articula reflexao, art, tradition em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como tradition e said.

texto: I shall suggest, on the contrary, that all communication relies, to a noticeable extent on evoking knowledge that we cannot tell, and that all our knowledge of mental processes, like feelings or conscious intellectual activities, is based on a knowledge which we cannot tell.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Polanyi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I shall suggest, on the contrary, that all communication relies, to a noticeable extent on evokin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, knowledge, feelings pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de feelings.

texto: A steady recognition that the evils which prevent the fullness of moral development are precisely the elements which are also the source of the power that gives existence to whatever moral accomplishments we see about us may eventually lead us to a tolerance we grant to the internal-combustion engine: it is noisy and smelly, and occasionally, it refuses to start, but it is what gets us to wherever we get.We must somehow learn to understand and so to tolerate- not destroy- the free society.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Michael Polanyi sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “A steady recognition that the evils which prevent the fullness of moral development are precisely…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, free-society, morality, útil em momentos de morality.

frase_original: A steady recognition that the evils which prevent the fullness of moral development are precisely the elements which are also the source of the power that gives existence to whatever moral accomplishments we see about us may eventually lead us to a tolerance we grant to the internal-combustion engine: it is noisy and smelly, and occasionally, it refuses to start, but it is what gets us to wherever we get.We must somehow learn to understand and so to tolerate- not destroy- the free society.

texto: We do not accept a religion because it offers us certain rewards. The only thing that a religion can offer us is to be just what it, in itself, is: a greater meaning in ourselves, in our lives, and in our grasp of the nature of things...a religion exists for us only if, like a piece of poetry, it carries us away. It is not in any sense a 'hypothesis.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Polanyi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “We do not accept a religion because it offers us certain rewards. The only thing that a religion …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, meaning, religion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de religion.
